structure.govukComponent: Text input (label govuk-label--m, outside the fieldset)
Fieldset (legend govuk-fieldset__legend--m) wrapping the address block

govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (class inventory + structure eval)

govukComponents.usage: Consignor or exporter name, Address line 1/2/3, City or town, Postcode or ZIP code, Telephone number, Email address

One govuk-label per field (9 total: name, address line 1/2/3, city or town, postcode, telephone, country, email). Only the name-field label carries the --m (medium) modifier; the other 8 are plain govuk-label. The fieldset legend is a separate element (govuk-fieldset__legend--m), not a label.
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (class inventory eval: main region has 9 label.govuk-label, 1 of which is label--m; 1 legend.govuk-fieldset__legend--m)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: main content region class inventory: govuk-!-width-one-half, govuk-body, govuk-button, govuk-fieldset, govuk-fieldset__legend, govuk-fieldset__legend--m, govuk-form-group, govuk-grid-column-full, govuk-grid-row, govuk-heading-xl, govuk-input, govuk-label, govuk-label--m, govuk-link, govuk-select
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Entire page is built from stock GOV.UK Design System classes — no custom widgets, no third-party libraries (no accessible-autocomplete), no bespoke tables/grids.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: None. The <main> region is 100% govuk-* prefixed. This page can be rebuilt one-for-one inside the govuk-frontend toolbox (Nunjucks textInput/select/button macros).
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (non-govuk class filter returned empty)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Reference-number header partial (common/referenceNumberHeaderDiv) rendering 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057 - CHEDD' as the section caption above H1

nonStandardPatterns.concern: Minor — it functions as the GOV.UK section caption but is a bespoke partial, not the standard govuk-caption-xl/-l element.

fields.name: company-name
fields.label: Consignor or exporter name
fields.conditionalOn: Label reads 'Consignor or exporter name' when a notification is in context (CHED-D consignor with notification -> labelKey CONSIGNOR_OR_EXPORTER_NAME); autocomplete='organization'; max 255 chars
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (a11y snapshot textbox ref=e60, value 'Linus George Ltd'). Requiredness legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:26 COMPANY_NAME Joi.string().max(255).empty('').required(). QA corroboration: page object exposes this control via accessible-name 'name' (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:7-9); driven from DEFAULT_CONFIG.consignorAddress.name='Linus George Ltd' (ched-d-workflows.ts:101)

fields.name: address-line-1
fields.label: Address line 1
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 48 (fill '101 Main St', id=address-line-1); also trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 ref=e65. Requiredness legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:30 ADDRESS_LINE_1 Joi.string().max(255).empty('').required()

fields.name: address-line-2
fields.label: Address line 2 (optional)
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (a11y snapshot ref=e68, value 'Suite 2'). Optional legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:34 ADDRESS_LINE_2 Joi.string().max(255).allow('') — no .required(), no ANY_REQUIRED message. Label carries '(optional)' suffix (template economic_operator_create.html:95)

fields.name: address-line-3
fields.label: Address line 3 (optional)
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (a11y snapshot ref=e71, value 'Building 2'). Optional legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:37 ADDRESS_LINE_3 Joi.string().max(255).allow('') — no .required(). Label carries '(optional)' suffix (template economic_operator_create.html:105)

fields.name: city-or-town
fields.label: City or town
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (fill 'Springfield', id=city-or-town). Requiredness legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:40 CITY_OR_TOWN Joi.string().max(58).empty('').required() — note the tighter 58-char cap

fields.name: postcode
fields.label: Postcode or ZIP code
fields.conditionalOn: autocomplete='postal-code'; max 32 chars. Visible label is 'Postcode or ZIP code' though the validation label constant is 'Post code'
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (a11y snapshot ref=e77). Optional legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:49 POSTCODE Joi.string().max(32).allow('') — no .required(). Validation label constant POSTCODE='Post code' (labels/en.js:81) differs from the visible label 'Postcode or ZIP code' (template economic_operator_create.html:125) — see discrepancies. observedValue '10000' is a QA fill, not a rendered-trace read: DEFAULT_CONFIG.consignorAddress.postcode='10000' (ched-d-workflows.ts:106; also a11y spec :100), driven via page object inputPostcodeOrZipCode (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:27-29)

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Telephone number
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (a11y snapshot ref=e80, control inventory type=tel). Requiredness legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:52 TELEPHONE Joi.string().max(30).empty('').required(). Validation label constant TELEPHONE='Telephone' (labels/en.js:97) differs from the visible label 'Telephone number' (template economic_operator_create.html:135) — see discrepancies. observedValue '01100100100' is a QA fill: DEFAULT_CONFIG.consignorAddress.telephone (ched-d-workflows.ts:107; a11y spec :101), driven via inputTelephoneNumber (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:31-33)

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.conditionalOn: First option 'Please select your country' is the empty placeholder. Full list is 254 options (253 countries/territories). Note: no single 'United Kingdom' option — the UK is split into England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland (inserted between 'United Arab Emirates' and 'United States Minor Outlying Islands'). Reference data: the new app should source this from a reference-data / countries service, not hardcode it. Option VALUES are ISO codes (the QA suite selects by value): e.g. Afghanistan='AF'; the four UK nations are England='GB-ENG', Northern Ireland='GB-NIR', Scotland='GB-SCT', Wales='GB-WLS' (types/country.ts:238-241).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (a11y snapshot combobox ref=e83, 254 options counted). Requiredness legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:56 COUNTRY Joi.string().valid(...countries).empty('').required() — mandatory AND value-validated against the allowed country list. observedValue 'England' (submit value 'GB-ENG') is a QA fill: DEFAULT_CONFIG.consignorAddress.country=country.England (ched-d-workflows.ts:108; a11y spec :102), driven via dropdownCountry.selectOption (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:35-37, 55); ISO option values from types/country.ts:238-241

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.conditionalOn: input type='email'; lowercased + email-format validated; max 255 chars
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 47 (a11y snapshot ref=e86, control inventory type=email). Requiredness/format legacy-confirmed: economic_operator.js:44 EMAIL Joi.string().lowercase().email({tlds:{allow:false}}).max(255).empty('').required() — mandatory, lowercased, RFC-format (TLD list not enforced), max 255. observedValue 'techsolutions@example.com' is a QA fill: DEFAULT_CONFIG.consignorAddress.email (ched-d-workflows.ts:109; a11y spec :103), driven via inputEmailAddress (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:39-41)

validationMessages.message: Enter a consignor or exporter name
validationMessages.trigger: Submit with the name field empty (CHED-D consignor with notification in context)
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/economic_operator.js:26,28 (COMPANY_NAME required, ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(labels[`${type.labelKey}_NAME`], type.article)). type.labelKey='CONSIGNOR_OR_EXPORTER', article='a' for consignor-with-notification (economic_operator.js:79-90). Label: labels/en.js:30 CONSIGNOR_OR_EXPORTER_NAME='Consignor or exporter name'. Helper: messages/en.js:31-33 requiredMessage=(label,article)=>`Enter ${article} ${label.toLowerCase()}`. Not observed rendered (both evidence traces had 0 errors) — copy is source-exact, not trace-confirmed.

validationMessages.message: Consignor or exporter name must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:26-27 (Joi.string().max(255); STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels[`${type.labelKey}_NAME`],255)). Label: labels/en.js:30 (CONSIGNOR_OR_EXPORTER_NAME). Helper: messages/en.js:3-5 maxLengthMessage=(label,max)=>`${label} must be ${max} characters or fewer`.

Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:30,32 (ADDRESS_LINE_1 required, ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(labels.ADDRESS_LINE_1,'an')). Label: labels/en.js:4 ADDRESS_LINE_1='Address line 1'. Helper: messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.message: Address line 1 must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:30-31 (max(255); STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.ADDRESS_LINE_1,255)). Label: labels/en.js:4. Helper: messages/en.js:3-5.

validationMessages.message: Address line 2 must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:34-35 (ADDRESS_LINE_2 optional allow(''); only STRING_MAX message defined -> maxLengthMessage(labels.ADDRESS_LINE_2,255)). Label: labels/en.js:5 ADDRESS_LINE_2='Address line 2'. Helper: messages/en.js:3-5. Field is optional (allow('')), so only a length error can fire.

validationMessages.message: Address line 3 must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:37-38 (ADDRESS_LINE_3 optional allow(''); STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.ADDRESS_LINE_3,255)). Label: labels/en.js:6 ADDRESS_LINE_3='Address line 3'. Helper: messages/en.js:3-5.

validationMessages.trigger: Submit with city or town empty
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:40,42 (CITY_OR_TOWN required, ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(labels.CITY_OR_TOWN,'a')). Label: labels/en.js:23 CITY_OR_TOWN='City or town'. Helper: messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.message: City or town must be 58 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:40-41 (max(58); STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.CITY_OR_TOWN,58)). Note the 58-char cap is tighter than the 255 on address lines. Label: labels/en.js:23. Helper: messages/en.js:3-5.

validationMessages.message: Post code must be 32 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Postcode exceeds 32 characters (postcode itself is optional)
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:49-50 (POSTCODE optional allow(''), max(32); STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.POSTCODE,32)). Label: labels/en.js:81 POSTCODE='Post code' (NB visible field label is 'Postcode or ZIP code' — mismatch, see discrepancies). Helper: messages/en.js:3-5.

Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:52,54 (TELEPHONE required, ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(labels.TELEPHONE,'a')). Label: labels/en.js:97 TELEPHONE='Telephone' (NB visible field label is 'Telephone number', constant TELEPHONE_NUMBER exists at labels/en.js:98 but the schema uses TELEPHONE — mismatch, see discrepancies). Helper: messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.message: Telephone must be 30 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:52-53 (max(30); STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.TELEPHONE,30)). Label: labels/en.js:97. Helper: messages/en.js:3-5.

Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:44,46 (EMAIL required, ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(labels.EMAIL_ADDRESS,'an')). Label: labels/en.js:46 EMAIL_ADDRESS='Email address'. Helper: messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.trigger: Email is not a valid format

Constant: messages/en.js:72 EMAIL_CORRECT_FORMAT='Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com'.

validationMessages.message: Email address must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:44-45 (max(255); STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.EMAIL_ADDRESS,255)). Label: labels/en.js:46. Helper: messages/en.js:3-5.

Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:56-57 (COUNTRY required, ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(labels.COUNTRY,'a')). Label: labels/en.js:34 COUNTRY='Country'. Helper: messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.trigger: Submitted country value is not in the allowed list (Joi.valid(...countries))
validationMessages.evidence: Verbatim from legacy source. Rule: economic_operator.js:56,58 (Joi.valid(...countries); ANY_VALID -> invalidMessage(labels.COUNTRY)). Label: labels/en.js:34. Helper: messages/en.js:19-21 invalidMessage=field=>field+' is not valid'.

openQuestions: All 9 fields, labels, the country option list, page title, caption, H1, back link and primary button were directly observed in traces (confirmed). Both evidence traces had 0 errors, so NO validation message was observed rendered — every entry in validationMessages is inferred from the Joi schema in ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/economic_operator.js and the en.js message/label helpers. The 38 error-bearing traces in the corpus were not mined here; a CHED-D consignor error trace would confirm the exact rendered copy and the error-summary ordering.

The country <select> holds 254 options (253 territories) and, notably, has NO 'United Kingdom' entry — the UK is represented as four separate options (England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland). The rebuild's reference-data / countries source must decide whether to preserve this four-nation split or use a single 'United Kingdom'.

Field-label vs validation-label mismatch: the visible label is 'Telephone number' but the validation constant labels it 'Telephone' (so the empty-field error reads 'Enter a telephone'). Likewise the visible label is 'Postcode or ZIP code' but the max-length error uses 'Post code'. The rebuild should align these.

The consignor NAME field label is context-dependent: 'Consignor or exporter name' when a notification is in context (observed here) vs 'Consignor name' when standalone/address-book (getMappedEconomicOperatorType, economic_operator.js:79-90). CHED-D in-journey always uses the 'or exporter' variant.
No govuk-hint text is present anywhere on this page (hint eval returned empty). No client-side 'required' attribute is set on any control — all mandatory-field enforcement is server-side via Joi.

This same template (economic_operator_create.html) is reused for consignee, importer, transporter, place-of-destination, etc. — the consignor is one instantiation. The rebuild may consolidate these into one reusable address/party form.

QA corroboration of the field set is complete and adds nothing new: the page object (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:4-58) exposes exactly the 9 controls already in the spec plus the 'Save and continue' button, and fillAddress (ConsignorCreationPage.ts:47-57) drives all 9 unconditionally. There is NO variant-only control the traces missed on this page — this raises confidence that the 9-field set is the whole page, not a lower bound.

The QA automation repo asserts NO validation messages for this page. grep across tests/notification/ched-d/*.spec.ts for consignor/exporter returns nothing — the consignor page is only ever *driven* (as a step in createChedDNotificationUpToTransportDetails, ched-d-workflows.ts:259-261), never *asserted*. So every entry in validationMessages remains inferred from the legacy Joi schema only; NONE is corroborated by either a trace or a test. A CHED-D consignor error trace (or a new negative test) is still needed to confirm the exact rendered copy and error-summary ordering.

Accessibility is an asserted requirement for this page: ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:105 runs an axe/WCAG scan (checkPageAccessibility 'Consignor Creation Page') after the page is filled, so passing automated accessibility checks is a human-stated rule the rebuild must also meet.

The Address test model (types/address.ts:1-11) types all 9 fields as required strings, but that is the test-data shape, not the app's server-side required-ness — it does not corroborate or contradict which fields the app treats as mandatory (lines 2/3 are labelled '(optional)' and postcode is optional per the legacy schema). Required-ness therefore still rests on the legacy Joi inference, not on any test assertion.

MANDATORINESS now legacy-confirmed (was inferred). The per-field required-ness is read directly from the authoritative frontend Joi schema (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/economic_operator.js:21-73): REQUIRED = company-name (:26), address-line-1 (:30), city-or-town (:40, max 58), email (:44), telephone (:52), country (:56); OPTIONAL = address-line-2 (:34 allow('')), address-line-3 (:37 allow('')), postcode (:49 allow(''), max 32). Max lengths: name/addr1/addr2/addr3/email = 255, city = 58, telephone = 30, postcode = 32. This is 'as the legacy system had it' — the rebuild may deliberately revisit, e.g. whether telephone should stay mandatory or postcode stay optional for GB addresses.

MODEL-LEVEL mandatoriness (legacy): the consignor party itself is @NotNull for the CHED-D validation groups. PartOne.java:192-214 annotates 'private EconomicOperator consignor' with @NotNull under NotificationCvedpEuFieldValidation (CHED-D = CVEDP/CED) and NotificationSingleCedValidation. Backend messages: consignor.not.null='Who is the consignor or exporter' (ValidationMessages.properties:12), singleced.consignor.not.null='Add a consignor' (:18). So the notification cannot be submitted without a consignor — this creation page exists to satisfy that model-level requirement. The eight per-field Joi rules above govern the address block within that mandatory party.

SHARED TEMPLATE — conditional controls no trace rendered (economic_operator_create.html is reused for consignor, consignee, importer, transporter, place-of-destination, etc.). Controls present in the template but NOT applicable to a CHED-D consignor: (a) 'Transporter authorisation number' text input, only when isCommercialTransporterType (:40-50); (b) a disabled Country <select> hard-wired to hidden value 'GB-NIR', only when isCheda AND isCommercialTransporterType (:158-165); (c) the govuk/select country macro variant, only when isOrganisationBranchAddress (:141-151) — the consignor uses the else branch's raw <select> with data/countriesSelectHighLow (:166-176). For the consignor these branches are inert. One conditional secondary action DOES apply as an alternative to the observed 'Return to search': 'Return to notification' link, rendered when returnToNotificationUrl is set (:34-38) instead of / alongside searchUrl (:28-32). Company-name label is context-driven: addressBookType present -> '<addressBookType> name'; else -> '<displayName> name' where displayName resolves to 'Consignor or exporter' for consignor-with-notification (template :52-74, economic_operator.js:79-90).
